result.stdout:
project-diagram
  A tool for creating a dependency diagram of the projects within a solution.

Usage:
  project-diagram [options]

Options:
  --version                                      Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information
  -s, --solution <solution> (REQUIRED)           The solution file to generate the project dependency diagram from.
  --options-file <options-file>                  The options file to load the project dependency diagram options from.
  -o, --output-filename <output-filename>        The output filename. Defaults to the name of the solution.
  --include-packages                             Include package dependencies in the diagram.
  --include-test-projects                        Include test projects (projects that reference NUnit, MSTest, xUnit or MbUnit).
  -i, --ignore <ignore>                          Ignore a specific project or package.
  --style <Boring|Plain|Scruffy>                 Sets the style of the dependency diagram.
  --size <Big|Huge|Normal|Small|Smaller>         Sets the size of the dependency diagram.
  --direction <LeftToRight|RightToLeft|TopDown>  Sets the direction of the dependency diagram.
  --color-modern                                 Color projects targeting modern frameworks (net5.0, netcore, netstandard).
  --show-targets                                 Show the target frameworks for each project.
  --image-type <Jpg|Pdf|Png|Svg>                 Sets the image type of the dependency diagram.
  --open-diagram                                 Open the dependency diagram after generation.
  --output-yuml                                  Saves the yuml for the dependency diagram to a file.
